steps.title: Identify the missing main verb
steps.content: The sentence has a subject (the International Astronomical Union) that needs a main verb to connect with the object and the trailing participial phrase. The blank must provide a finite verb to form the main clause.

steps.title: Match the requirement to the choices
steps.content: Choice A supplies would finally acknowledge, a finite verb that forms the main clause with the subject. The participial phrase naming a crater on Venus after her modifies this main action, showing how the acknowledgment occurred. Choices B, C, and D each provide a non-finite form (infinitive or participle) rather than a finite verb, leaving the sentence without a main clause. That matches choice A — A is correct.
